## Methodology
Polysulfone membranes were synthesized using non-solvent-induced phase separation (NIPS) with an automated robotic system (experiment index #28). A 12.0% w/w polysulfone solution was prepared in PolarClean solvent at room temperature (25??C) without additional heating. The solution was automatically mixed by the robotic system and allowed to rest for 1 day in a sample vial before processing.

## Casting Procedure
The polymer solution was drop-cast onto a substrate coupon with dry nitrogen flow applied from the side to minimize humidity effects. The solution was then blade-cast using the automated system. Immediately after blade casting (0.0 min wait time), the cast film was immersed in the NIPS bath to initiate phase separation. No nitrogen flow was applied after blade casting. The ambient relative humidity during synthesis was 54%.

## Notes
This experiment utilized full automation for solution preparation and casting, with specific humidity control measures (side nitrogen flow) to ensure reproducible membrane formation under controlled conditions.
